title: Mud on Denim
timeEstimate: 20–40 minutes; DRY first, then chemistry; indigo / ring-risk warnings
stainChemistry: Mud on denim is the textbook DRY-FIRST stain: wet mud + water = a bigger spread, dried mud + brush = clean lift. The recurring trap is reaching for a damp cloth on wet mud — that smears the soil into a wider ring and drives clay particles deeper into the denim weave. The right move is to LET MUD DRY FULLY first (hours or overnight), then brush off the bulk dry, then deal with the residual color. Denim adds two complications: indigo dye loss (hidden test before any solvent), and aggressive scrubbing on dark denim leaves a halo ring. Heavy clay-rich mud (red clay) carries iron that adds a rust-component shadow — that's harder than ordinary garden mud.
whyThisWorks: Letting mud dry fully turns it into brittle particles that brush off cleanly. Wet-cloth-first is the most common home failure on mud — water turns soil into a slurry that smears wider. After dry-brush, Dawn pretreat handles any oily / organic component. Cool wash plus AIR-DRY locks nothing. Iron-rich red-clay residue may need a separate mineral / oxalic pass, but skip chlorine bleach (strips indigo) and skip peroxide hidden-test failures.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): mud is mineral particulate (clay, silt, sand) + organic matter + sometimes iron oxides (red clay). Pro uses HEPA vacuum after dry + NSD + iron-spotter (oxalic acid) for red-clay residue + acetic acid neutralizing rinse.
defaultAssumption: Garden / playground / hike mud on denim. Fresh and wet = wait for it to dry, then chemistry. Already dry = brush + Dawn + cool wash. Red-clay regions = iron-residue may persist, oxalic acid spot for stubborn rust shadow.

spottingProtocol.agent: LET DRY — Don't Touch Wet Mud
spottingProtocol.instruction: If mud is still wet, LET IT DRY COMPLETELY before any chemistry. Wet mud + water = bigger spread. Wait for it to harden into brittle particles.
Once dry: stiff-brush the mud off the denim outdoors or over a sheet. HEPA vacuum residue. Most dried mud comes off this way.
Inside seam: dab 3% peroxide, blot with white cloth. Indigo transfers = skip peroxide. Light-wash denim usually passes; raw / dark / selvage often doesn't.
Dawn directly on residue. Soft toothbrush 5 min. Always blot, never rub — denim halos under aggressive scrubbing. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.agent: Oxalic Acid for Red-Clay Iron Residue
spottingProtocol.equipment: Bar Keepers Friend (oxalic acid) per label
spottingProtocol.instruction: If red-clay rust shadow remains: Bar Keepers Friend per label, light scrub, rinse THOROUGHLY. Hidden-seam test on dark denim first — oxalic acid can lighten indigo.
Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY only. NEVER hot dryer with residual color. NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly.

steps: 2. Stiff brush + HEPA vacuum dried particles.
3. Hidden-seam peroxide test on dark denim.
4. Dawn pretreat cool 5 min, soft toothbrush.
5. Oxalic acid (BKF) for red-clay iron residue, hidden test on dark denim.
products: Bar Keepers Friend (oxalic acid for red-clay rust)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets organic residue
warnings: DRY FIRST — wet mud + water = bigger spread
NEVER hot water — sets organic residue
NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly
Hidden-seam test on dark / raw denim before peroxide or oxalic
Always blot, never rub — denim halos
Red-clay regions: oxalic for iron shadow, hidden test mandatory

homeSolutions.agent: Let Mud Dry First — Don't Touch Wet
homeSolutions.instruction: Mud on denim is the classic DRY-FIRST stain. The recurring failure is grabbing a damp cloth and dabbing — water turns mud into a slurry that smears into a wider ring. Wait. Let the mud dry completely (hours, or overnight). Once it's brittle, it brushes off cleanly. THIS is the unlock — patience beats chemistry on mud.
Once dry: stiff-brush the mud off the denim outdoors or over a sheet (otherwise you spread dust around the room). HEPA vacuum the residue. Most dried mud comes off this way without any liquid.

Before any peroxide on dark or raw denim: dab 3% peroxide on an inside seam, blot with a white cloth. Indigo transfers = skip peroxide. Light-wash denim usually passes; raw / dark / selvage often doesn't.
Dawn dish soap directly on residue. Work in with a soft toothbrush for 5 minutes. Cool damp-cloth rinse. Always blot, never rub — rubbing causes a halo on dark denim.

homeSolutions.agent: Bar Keepers Friend for Red-Clay Iron Shadow
homeSolutions.instruction: If your mud was red clay (Georgia clay, terracotta soil), iron oxide leaves a rust-colored shadow even after Dawn. Bar Keepers Friend (oxalic acid powder, mix to paste with water) per label, light scrub, rinse THOROUGHLY. Hidden-seam test on dark denim first — oxalic can lighten indigo.
Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY only first time. NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly and leaves a halo. NEVER hot dryer with residual color.

steps: 1. LET DRY completely. Don't touch wet mud with water.
2. Stiff brush + HEPA vacuum dried particles outdoors / over sheet.
3. Hidden-seam peroxide test on dark denim (skip if indigo transfers).
4. Dawn pretreat cool 5 min, soft toothbrush.
5. Bar Keepers Friend (oxalic acid) for red-clay iron shadow — hidden test on dark denim.
6. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. NO chlorine bleach.
products: Bar Keepers Friend (oxalic acid for red-clay rust)
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if denim is raw / selvage / black-overdyed (pro). Stop if hidden-seam test shows indigo loss before BKF. Stop after 2 home cycles plateau.
warnings: DRY FIRST — wet mud + water = bigger spread
NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly
Hidden-seam test on dark / raw denim before peroxide or oxalic
Always blot, never rub — denim halos
Red-clay residue may need oxalic acid (BKF), bounded

neverDo: Never wet mud first — turns into slurry and spreads
Never chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly
Never rub denim — halos under aggressive scrubbing
Never use BKF without gloves — oxalic acid is a skin irritant
fiberSensitivities: Indigo / dark denim: hidden-seam test before peroxide and BKF
Raw / selvage denim: pro from minute one
Light-wash denim: most forgiving — full home protocol
Stretch denim with elastane: cool only — BKF can stress elastane

materialWarnings: DRY-FIRST is the entire unlock — wet mud + water spreads
No chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo
Red-clay iron residue is a separate sub-stain (oxalic acid)
Hidden test on dark denim before BKF — oxalic can lighten indigo

escalation.when: Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim. Hidden-seam test shows indigo loss before peroxide or oxalic. Wet mud was already smeared with damp cloth (spread into wider ring). Hot dryer was used on residual color. 2 home cycles plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Mud on [light-wash / dark / indigo / raw / selvage] denim, type [garden / clay / red-clay / playground], age [fresh wet / dried], home-treated with [DRY first + brush + Dawn + BKF for red-clay if applicable + cool wash]. No chlorine, no hot. [Residual shadow / indigo loss / wet smeared]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with denim experience plus iron-spotter capability for red-clay residue

contextQuestions: Wet mud (let dry first) or already dried in?
Garden soil, playground mud, or red clay (iron-rich)?
Light-wash, dark indigo, raw / selvage denim?
Has water been applied to wet mud (smeared into wider ring)?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: DRY-FIRST is the entire unlock on mud — patience beats chemistry. Red-clay regions add iron residue that needs oxalic acid (BKF). Indigo dye loss is the recurring denim risk. Most home failures are wet-cloth-first; the second-most-common is reaching for chlorine bleach which strips indigo unevenly.
